content.problemStatement:
Naveej is from a tribe that speaks some weird language - their alphabet consists of NNN distinct characters.
He has an array A=[A1,A2,…,AN]A = [A_1, A_2, \dots, A_N]A=[A1​,A2​,…,AN​], where AiA_iAi​ denotes the number of occurrences of the iii-th character with him.

He wants to make a palindromic string using **all** the characters he has (every character he has must be used in this string).

In order to make this possible, he can perform the following operation:


Select an iii (1≤i≤N)(1 \le i \le N)(1≤i≤N) and convert all occurrences of iii-th alphabet to any other alphabet of his choice.
Note that Naveej just wants to be able to make any palindrome, as long as every character is used. For example, if N=2N = 2N=2 and A=[2,2]A = [2, 2]A=[2,2] and we consider the characters to be aaa and bbb, he can make both abbaabbaabba and baabbaabbaab, but abaabaaba is not allowed because it uses only 333 characters.

Find the minimum number of operations required such that Naveej can make a palindromic string using all the characters he has. It can be proven that there always exists at least one sequence of operations allowing for the formation of a palindrome.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN - the size of the alphabet.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers: A1,A2,...,ANA_1, A_2, ..., A_NA1​,A2​,...,AN​, where AiA_iAi​ is the number of occurrences of the iii-th character with Naveej.


Output Format
For each test case, output a single line containing one integer - the minimum number of operations required so that Naveej can make a palindromic string using all the characters he has.
constraints: 1 ≤ N ≤ 2·10^5 (sum of N over all test cases ≤ 2·10^5)
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N – the size of the alphabet. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, …, AN, where Ai is the number of occurrences of the i‑th character.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the minimum number of operations required so that all characters can be rearranged into a palindrome.

sampleTestCases.explanation: First test case: only one character with even count, O=0, target=0, answer=0. Second test case: odd counts are 3 and 1 (O=2), total length is 8 (even), target=0, answer=(2-0)/2=1.

solution.approach: A palindrome can be formed iff the multiset of character frequencies contains at most one odd frequency. Let O be the number of characters whose frequency is odd and let S be the total number of characters. The parity of S equals the parity of O (because the sum of odd numbers is odd exactly when their count is odd). The required number of odd frequencies after all operations is therefore target = S mod 2 (0 for even total length, 1 for odd total length). One operation can merge two odd‑frequency characters: the source disappears (removing one odd) and the target becomes even (removing another odd). Thus each operation can reduce O by 2. No operation can reduce O by 1. Consequently the minimum number of operations is (O - target) / 2. The algorithm for each test case is: 1) count O, 2) compute S, 3) target = S % 2, 4) answer = (O - target) / 2. This runs in O(N) time per test case and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over the whole input.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        odd = 0
        total = 0
        for _ in range(N):
            x = int(next(it))
            total += x
            if x & 1:
                odd += 1
        target = total & 1
        ans = (odd - target) // 2
        out_lines.append(str(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
